summary: Stellar 10/30/80 performance highlighted by a soaring Other One and crisp audience recording that captures the show’s acoustic brilliance and electric fire.
review: The concert is widely praised for its diverse setlist, tight playing and memorable moments – especially the drums‑>space‑>Other One sequence with guest drummer Billy Cobham and the smooth Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance.  The Jim Wise front‑of‑house tape is described as beautiful and well‑balanced despite being audience‑heavy, making it one of the best available recordings of this three‑show run.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5‑star ratings and extol both the musical performance and the audio quality; only one modestly negative comment about audience heaviness tempers the overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Jim Wise's front‑of‑house tape is praised as clear, crisp and beautifully balanced, though a few reviewers note it can be heavy on audience ambience and the first‑set mix is slightly funky.

standout_songs: The Other One (drums > space > Other One)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set shines with acoustic gems and tight electric numbers, the second builds momentum toward a powerful Lost Sailor‑Saint pairing, and the third delivers an epic climax highlighted by the guest‑drummer jam and a whirlwind Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is called beautiful; his solos on Dark Hollow, Ripple and The Other One are described as astonishing and he even walks in rhythm during the jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass work is praised for sneaking into Heaven Help and cueing the band for The Other One; overall his playing is noted as solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s electric‑piano solo on Dark Hollow receives special mention, adding sparkle to the acoustic set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy Cobham joins Bill and Mickey for a legendary drums > space > Other One segment; the drumming overall is highlighted as a high point of the night.
